sections.paragraph:
Original: קישׁון

Transliteration: qı̂yshôn

Phonetic: kee-shone'

BDB Definition:

Kishon = "winding"
  1. a river in central Palestine; scene of the defeat of Sisera by the Israelites in the time of the judges and the destruction of the prophets of Baal by Elijah

Origin: from H6983

TWOT entry: None

Part(s) of speech: Proper Name Location
